source_jobs.title: Cloud and ML Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
CLOUD AND ML INFRASTRUCTURE ENGINEER

$120k–$190k + equity

Somerville, MA



About Glimpse

The last decade has been incredibly exciting for electric mobility. However, the electrification of the transportation industry has scaled while relying on an insufficient set of quality control tools. This has led to uncomfortably high rates of battery pack failure and multi-billion-dollar safety recalls. In our view, every headline of electric vehicles https://www.npr.org/2023/03/28/1166625069/batteries-are-catching-fire-at-sea and e-bikes https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2022/nov/14/new-york-e-bike-batteries-fires-delivery-workers catching fire is a major impediment towards an all-electric future.



After having spent a few years in the trenches of battery and electric vehicle factories at Tesla and Toyota, Glimpse’s cofounders developed a vision for a one-stop-shop battery Quality Management Solution, which they actualized through the launch of the Glimpse Portal https://app.glimp.se/ in March 2024. Today, Glimpse counts some of the world’s largest battery producers and OEMs, such as Lucid Motors and NASA, among its customers.



Role

Glimpse has built a powerful technology stack that uses high-powered edge computing for compression and noise reduction, cloud infrastructure for storage and AI for feature detection in CT images. As we scale our platform and expand its capabilities, we are looking for exceptional engineers to help us build the infrastructure to provide scalability and maintainability.



This person should have experience in maintaining complex systems and be familiar with MLOps workflows, training infrastructure, and AWS.



Relevant skills

 - Experience with AWS and production infrastructure for ML/AI workloads, especially managing GPU-compute workflows

 - Experience with designing monitoring and alerting systems for algorithms

 - Strong python skills, especially in performance sensitive applications

 - Understanding of compliance frameworks such as SOC 2, NIST 800-171, and how to implement automated controls to match these requirements.



Our culture and values

A working draft of our culture and values aspirations can be found here https://glimpse-eng.notion.site/Glimpse-values-culture-and-guidelines-117227f570a846c59bb89b36f077402e.


You’re fired up by:

 - Making electric mobility safer, more affordable, and less wasteful

 - Working at the intersection of sales, operations, and customer success

 - Relentlessly getting things done and taking ownership for and pride in your work

 - Delighting Glimpse’s customers



You have:

*If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply!

 - Excellent python developer, with experience in performance critical applications

 - Experience building and maintaining high reliability platforms on AWS

 - 2-4 years of experience

 - US citizenship or a green card



Nice-to-have:

 - Experience with Rust and other high performance languages.

 - Experience at high-growth, fast-paced startups



Location

We’re headquartered in Greentown Labs in Somerville, MA. As an early-stage startup working on hardware and software, we have a strong preference for being in the office more often than not. However, we’re happy to consider flexibility options for exceptional candidates.



Interview process

 - Phone screen with recruiter (15-20 minutes)

 - Intro call with hiring manager (30 minutes)

 - Live technical interview session (1.5 hour)

 - On-site interviews (half a day)

 - Two reference checks

 - Background check



We offer

 - Incredible growth prospects in a well-funded Series A climate company

 - Medical, dental, and vision benefits for employee and dependents

 - 401(k) plan with a company match

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 12-week parental leave for both parents

source_jobs.title: Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
MARKETING LEAD

Base: $100,000 - $160,000 + equity | Flexible for exceptional candidates who exceed our requirements.

Location: Somerville, MA (Greentown Labs)



About Glimpse

While working in battery and EV factories at Tesla and Toyota, Glimpse's founders realized that the electrification of the transportation industry had scaled while relying on an insufficient set of quality control tools - leading to uncomfortably high rates of battery pack failure and multi-billion-dollar safety recalls.

Leveraging X-ray CT scanning - the industrial equivalent of a CAT scan - they launched the Glimpse Portal® in 2024, enabling world-class customers such as NASA, Lucid Motors, and Archer Aviation to tightly control their battery quality at scale. After quadrupling revenue in 2025, Glimpse is now expanding into new use cases and manufacturing industries that rely too heavily on an outdated CT scanning technology stack to control the quality of their parts.



About the Role

Glimpse has built its customer base through direct outreach, a strong network, and trade shows. That's gotten us here. What gets us to the next stage is building an inbound marketing engine - one that makes Glimpse the first name engineers and quality managers think of when they need a CT scan or a CT data platform.

We are expanding into new industries and new customer types, across both our scanning services and our on-premise software. We need the marketing stack to match that ambition.

You will be Glimpse's first dedicated marketing hire, reporting to the Head of Business Development and working closely with our Head of Sales and CEO. You will own our channel strategy, content creation, and execution - including a near-term website refresh, SEO/AEO, paid search, and analytics - building most of it from scratch. No marketing team above you, no one to hand things off to. If that sounds exciting rather than intimidating, we would love to talk.



Our culture and values

A working draft of our culture and values aspirations can be found here https://glimpse-eng.notion.site/Glimpse-values-culture-and-guidelines-117227f570a846c59bb89b36f077402e.



You're Fired Up By:

 - Building - you find a blank canvas more energizing than an inherited playbook

 - Making technical products accessible to people who don't yet know they need them

 - Knowing that your work connects to something real - the parts Glimpse helps inspect end up in EVs, aircraft, and medical devices, and quality failures have consequences

 - Relentlessly getting things done and taking ownership for and pride in your work



You Have:

If you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply.

 - 3-5 years of B2B marketing experience, with real ownership of at least one channel or function

 - Hands-on experience with SEO/AEO, paid search, and content marketing

 - Experience leading or contributing to a website redesign, including copy and wireframes

 - Strong writing and design skills

 - Comfort with marketing analytics - you can build a dashboard to track channel performance

 - Startup or small company experience where you wore multiple hats

 - US citizenship or green card



Nice-to-Have:

 - Background in manufacturing, deep tech, SaaS, or scientific instruments

 - Experience with Google Ads or LinkedIn ads

 - Familiarity with AI search optimization techniques

 - Experience with CRM tools

 - Trade show or technical conference planning



Location

We are headquartered at Greentown Labs in Somerville, MA. Given the hands-on nature of this role, we have a strong preference for candidates in the Boston metro area. We are happy to discuss flexibility options for exceptional candidates.



Interview Process

 - Initial screen with recruiter (15-20 minutes)

 - Intro call with hiring manager (30 minutes)

 - Live marketing strategy whiteboard session (60 minutes)

 - Take-home content exercise (time-boxed to 3-4 hours)

 - On-site interviews at Greentown Labs (half day)

 - Two

source_jobs.title: Senior Edge Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR EDGE INFRASTRUCTURE ENGINEER

$140k - $200k, 0.05% - 0.1%

Somerville, MA



About Glimpse

The last decade has been incredibly exciting for electric mobility. However, the electrification of the transportation industry has scaled while relying on an insufficient set of quality control tools. This has led to uncomfortably high rates of battery pack failure and multi-billion-dollar safety recalls. In our view, every headline of electric vehicles https://www.npr.org/2023/03/28/1166625069/batteries-are-catching-fire-at-sea and e-bikes https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2022/nov/14/new-york-e-bike-batteries-fires-delivery-workers catching fire is a major impediment towards an all-electric future.



After having spent a few years in the trenches of battery and electric vehicle factories at Tesla and Toyota, Glimpse’s cofounders developed a vision for a one-stop-shop battery Quality Management Solution, which they actualized through the launch of the Glimpse Portal https://app.glimp.se/ in March 2024. Today, Glimpse counts some of the world’s largest battery producers and OEMs, such as Lucid Motors and NASA, among its customers.



Role

We're looking for a sharp, pragmatic Software Engineer ready to take on a unique challenge: building high-throughput data processing systems that operate autonomously at the edge and in demanding environments. This role blends software engineering, DevOps, and hands-on compute hardware interaction to deliver systems capable of processing terabytes of data per hour.

You’ll play a key role in designing and implementing scalable, reliable infrastructure that bridges the physical and digital worlds. If you thrive in solving real-world problems with code, love Python, and enjoy working close to the metal, this role is for you.



Relevant skills

NixOS, Linux, Python, Rust, Networking, GPU accelerated computing



Our culture and values

A working draft of our culture and values aspirations can be found here https://glimpse-eng.notion.site/Glimpse-values-culture-and-guidelines-117227f570a846c59bb89b36f077402e.


You’re fired up by:

 - Making electric mobility safer, more affordable, and less wasteful

 - Working at the intersection of compute hardware and software, where innovation meets hard problems.

 - Relentlessly getting things done and taking ownership for and pride in your work

 - Helping us define our engineering culture and best practices.



You have:

*If you don’t meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply!

 - 5+ years as a strong software/hardware engineer with experience deploying code at the edge

 - Expertise with Linux and NixOS as a declarative system configuration

 - Fluency in Python with a deep understanding of language and ecosystem

 - Authorization to work in the US



Nice-to-have

 - Proven experience with DevOps practices and Infrastructure as Code (IaC) frameworks

 - Some level of fluency with Rust

 - Experience working on high-reliability computing platforms

 - Understanding of computer networking principles and infrastructure

 - Ability to use accelerated computing frameworks such as CUDA

 - Experience at high-growth, fast-paced startups



Location

We’re headquartered in Greentown Labs in Somerville, MA. As an early-stage startup working on hardware and software, we have a strong preference for being in the office more often than not. However, we’re happy to consider flexibility options for exceptional candidates.



Interview process

 - Phone screen with recruiter (15-20 minutes)

 - Intro call with hiring manager (30 minutes)

 - Live coding session (~1.5 hours)

 - On-site interviews (half a day)

 - Two reference checks

 - Background check



We offer

 - Incredible growth prospects in a cutting edge industry

 - Medical, dental, and vision benefits for employee and dependents

 - 401(k) plan with a company match

 - Unlimited PTO

 - 12-week parental leave for both parents

notes: Glimpse is an electric mobility company with a strong focus on engineering and infrastructure.
